hook.scenario:
Reading DI questions is exactly like reading a Zepto operational dashboard during peak IPL hours. You have a massive table of riders, zones, and delivery times:

| Zone | Active Riders | Avg Delivery Time |
|------|---------------|-------------------|
| Kormangala | 450 | 14m |
| Indiranagar| 320 | 18m |

Your job isn't to calculate every single cell—it's to spot the bottleneck instantly using rough ratios and percentage jumps.

mental_model.intuition: DI is just basic percentages, ratios, and averages hidden inside visual noise.
mental_model.description: Data Interpretation tests your ability to extract relevant data from tables, bar graphs, pie charts, and line graphs, and perform quick approximations rather than precise calculations.

formula.statement: Visual Data -> Extract Numbers -> Apply Basic Arithmetic (Percentage Change, Ratio, Average). Focus heavily on visual approximation.
formula.explain: In a pie chart, 1% = 3.6 degrees. For line graphs, the slope indicates the rate of growth. Don't compute exact values unless options are dangerously close.

shortcuts.title: The Approximation Rule
shortcuts.trick: If options are spaced out (e.g., 10%, 15%, 20%), round numbers heavily. 412/1987 becomes 400/2000 = 1/5 = 20%.
shortcuts.example: Finding 18.2% of 498? Just find 20% of 500 (100) and subtract a little.

shortcuts.title: Pie Chart Degree to Percentage
shortcuts.trick: To convert degrees to percentage, divide by 3.6. To convert percentage to degrees, multiply by 3.6.

shortcuts.title: Ratio Stacking
shortcuts.trick: When comparing two huge fractions A/B and C/D, compare the percentage growth of numerators vs denominators, or cross-multiply.
shortcuts.example: Compare 145/290 vs 160/310. First is exactly 50%. Second is > 50% (since 155 is 50%). So 160/310 is larger.

common_mistakes.title: Calculating Everything
common_mistakes.explanation: Don't calculate values for all years/categories. Only calculate what the specific question needs.

common_mistakes.title: Confusing 'Percentage More' with 'Percentage Of'
common_mistakes.explanation: If A is 150 and B is 100. A is 150% OF B, but A is 50% MORE than B.

recall_questions.q: A pie chart sector is 108 degrees. What percentage of the total does it represent?
If Company A's revenue grew from 400 to 500, what is the percentage growth?

oa_questions.question: A table shows revenue: 2021 (420M), 2022 (480M), 2023 (550M). What is the approximate average annual percentage growth rate?
In a pie chart of expenses (Total = 50,000), 'Marketing' is represented by 54 degrees. What is the expenditure on Marketing?
